title: Implementation Verification Record
description: Written to .sc4sap/program/{PROG}/verification.json during create-program Phase 4 (check_syntax, activate) and Phase 5 (unit_test, atc). Each step records a status plus concrete evidence (tool output summary), so the Phase 6 reviewer and the Phase 8 report can trust the claims without re-running the tools.

check_syntax.description: Result of CheckSyntax on the MAIN program (single check, not per include). PASS/FAIL only — a backend outage on this step blocks the pipeline (Phase 6 records state.json as blocked), it does not SKIP the gate.

activate.description: Activation result — evidence should cite GetInactiveObjects returning 0 entries for the program's object set. PASS/FAIL only — a backend outage on this step blocks the pipeline (Phase 6 records state.json as blocked), it does not SKIP the gate.

unit_test.description: RunUnitTest / GetUnitTestResult outcome. SKIPPED when Phase 5 skip conditions matched (Procedural paradigm, or testing scope = none) — SKIPPED here is a normal, non-blocking outcome.

atc.description: GetAtcFindings outcome on the created objects. SKIPPED when the backend does not support ATC checks — SKIPPED here is a normal, non-blocking outcome.

step.description: Used by unit_test and atc only. These two steps are conditional on backend/project support, so SKIPPED is a legitimate, non-blocking outcome as long as evidence carries the reason.

status.description: Step outcome for unit_test/atc. SKIPPED requires the reason in evidence and does not block Phase 6/Phase 8.

evidence.description: Concrete proof: tool name + result summary (e.g. 'RunUnitTest: 6/6 methods green', 'GetAtcFindings: 0 priority-1/2 findings'), or the skip reason (e.g. 'SKIPPED: Procedural paradigm, no test class expected').

step-strict.description: Used by check_syntax and activate only. These two are the unconditional machine-verification gates (verification-policy.md chain steps 1-2) that Phase 6 step 1 and the Phase 8 hard gate both require to have actually PASSed — SKIPPED is deliberately excluded from the enum so a backend outage on either step surfaces as FAIL + a blocked pipeline, never as a silent pass-through.

status.description: Step outcome for check_syntax/activate. No SKIPPED — these steps must actually run and resolve to PASS or FAIL.

evidence.description: Concrete proof: tool name + result summary (e.g. 'CheckSyntax(ZMM_STOCK_RPT): 0 errors 0 warnings', 'GetInactiveObjects: 0 entries for ZMM_STOCK_RPT*').
